title: Low-Dose Widefield Fluorescence (photon-starved Airy deconvolution)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical domain literature for this specific principle.

E.description: Low-Dose Widefield Fluorescence (photon-starved Airy deconvolution): fluorescence widefield produces the measurement through a 2-node primitive DAG K.psf.airy -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and Poisson signal noise + Gaussian read noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D intensity. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~8; calibration-level mismatch (dz_nm, sigma_bg, shot_noise_variance) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y = [PSF_Airy conv f] + n, n Poisson-dominated at <50 photons/px
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter of interest (density / complex field / lifetime / normals depending on spec).
E.observation_y: Measured intensity or photon counts from the imaging system.

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D intensity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 8); dz_nm dominates the stability cliff; sigma_bg and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Poisson signal noise + gaussian read noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + analytic regularisation [Anscombe-BM3D] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [CARE-UNet-Poisson, Noise2Noise]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_read^2 + alpha * signal) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |dz_nm| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
